== Notes for [[Item:Q88237|Q88237]] ==

Stratonice of Thrace married Antigonus I Monophthalamos, King of Phrygia, son of Philip of Elimea, 0350 B.C.1,2 Stratonice of Thrace was born 0370 B.C.. She was the daughter of King Corrhaeus of Thrace.1,2 Stratonice of Thrace was the mother of Demetrius I Poliorcetes, King of Phrygia and Macedonia; the son of Antigonus by his wife, Stratonice, the daughter of Corrhaeus.3 Family Antigonus I Monophthalamos, King of Phrygia b. 0382 B.C., d. 0301 B.C. Child Demetrius I Poliorcetes, King of Phrygia and Macedonia+ b. 0348 B.C., d. 0283 B.C.1,2

Citations [S204] Roderick W. Stuart, RfC, 427-81. [S931] A.H. Clough, Plutarch's Lives. [S931] A.H. Clough, Plutarch's Lives, DEMETRIUS.

http://homepages.rootsweb.ancestry.com/~cousin/html/p113.htm
--------------------
Stratonice (Ancient Greek: Στρατoνίκη, Stratoníkē, lived 4th century BC) was daughter of Corrhaeus (Κορῥαῖος, Korrhaĩos, a Macedonian otherwise unknown), and wife of Antigonus, king of Asia, by whom she became the mother of two sons, Demetrius Poliorcetes and Philip, who died in 306 BC.1 In 316 BC she is mentioned as entering into negotiations with Docimus, when that general was shut up with the other adherents of Perdiccas, in a fortress of Phrygia: but having induced him to quit his stronghold, she caused him to be seized and detained as a prisoner.2 After the battle of Ipsus she fled from Cilicia (where she had awaited the issue of the campaign) with her son Demetrius to Salamis in Cyprus, 301 BC.3 Here she probably died, as we hear nothing of her when the island fell into the power of Ptolemy some years afterwards.
--------------------
'''
=Stratonice=
'''

Stratonice (Ancient Greek: Στρατoνίκη Stratoníkē; fl. 4th century BC) was daughter of Corrhaeus (Κορῥαῖος, Korrhaĩos, a Macedonian otherwise unknown), and wife of Antigonus, king of Asia, by whom she became the mother of two sons, Demetrius Poliorcetes and Philip, who died in 306 BC. In 316 BC she is mentioned as entering into negotiations with Docimus, when that general was shut up with the other adherents of Perdiccas, in a fortress of Phrygia: but having induced him to quit his stronghold, she caused him to be seized and detained as a prisoner. After the battle of Ipsus she fled from Cilicia (where she had awaited the issue of the campaign) with her son Demetrius to Salamis in Cyprus, 301 BC. Here she probably died, as nothing is mentioned of her when the island fell into the power of Ptolemy some years afterwards.

'''Source : '''

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stratonice_(wife_of_Antigonus)

RESEARCH NOTES:
Princess of Thrace

SOURCE NOTES:
http://www.american-pictures.com/genealogy/persons/per01231.htm#0

Stratonice (wife of Antigonus)
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Stratonice (in Greek St?ato????; lived 4th century BC) was daughter of Corrhaeus (a Macedonian otherwise unknown), and wife of Antigonus, king of Asia, by whom she became the mother of two sons, Demetrius Poliorcetes and Philip, who died in 306 BC.1 In 316 BC she is mentioned as entering into negotiations with Docimus, when that general was shut up with the other adherents of Perdiccas, in a fortress of Phrygia: but having induced him to quit his stronghold, she caused him to be seized and detained as a prisoner.2 After the battle of Ipsus she fled from Cilicia (where she had awaited the issue of the campaign) with her son Demetrius to Salamis in Cyprus, 301 BC.3 Here she probably died, as we hear nothing of her when the island fell into the power of Ptolemy some years afterwards.
[edit]

References

* Smith, William (editor); Dictionary of Greek and Roman Biography and Mythology, "Stratonice (2)", Boston, (1867)

[[Category:Possibly derived from wikipedia]]
[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
